title: festuca glauca var. cinerea
intro: Festuca glauca var. cinerea, also known as gray blue fescue, is a type of grass that is native to North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Festuca glauca var. cinerea include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and soothing coughs and colds. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used as a tea or tincture to harness its medicinal properties, or it may be applied topically as a salve or poultice.
